info.title: Volaura API
info.description: Verified Competency Platform — Assessment, Matching, Events

get.description: Real health check: verifies Supabase is reachable and LLM keys are set.

Return whether signup is open. Public — no auth required.

open_signup=True  → anyone can register (dev mode, or fully launched)
open_signup=False → invite code required (controlled beta)

post.description:
Validate a beta invite code. Returns {valid: bool}.

Uses constant-time comparison (hmac.compare_digest) to prevent timing attacks.
If BETA_INVITE_CODE is empty, always returns valid=False (gate is armed but
misconfigured — prevents silent bypass when code is not yet set on Railway).
Register a new user via Supabase Auth (anon key — OWASP HIGH-05 fix).
Login with email + password (anon key — OWASP HIGH-05 fix).

get.description: Get current user info from JWT.

delete.description:
Permanently delete the current user's account and all associated data.

Steps:
1. Delete auth user via Supabase admin API (cascades to profiles, sessions, etc.)
2. Return success — client must call supabase.auth.signOut() to clear local state.

GDPR compliance: all user data removed. This action is irreversible.

post.description:
Logout — revoke token server-side (BUG-016 fix).

Calls Supabase admin sign_out with the current JWT. Because auth validation
uses admin.auth.get_user(token), any subsequent request with this token will
fail with 401 immediately — no need for a local blacklist table.

Scope: "global" — invalidates ALL sessions for this user (all devices).

Create a confirmed test user and return a real session token.

Protected by E2E_TEST_SECRET — returns 404 when not configured (safe default).
Uses admin API to bypass email confirmation, then signs in for a real session.

Bridge a user from a standalone Supabase project into shared project.

Returns a shared-project JWT the caller can use for all /api/character/*
endpoints. See module docstring for full flow.

get.description: Get the current authenticated user's profile.

post.description: Create a profile for the current user (called after registration).

Create a one-use verification link for a professional.

Only the professional themselves can request verification links.
The link is sent to an expert who rates the professional's competency.
Token is valid for 7 days, single-use.

get.description:
List professionals who have opted in to org discovery (visible_to_orgs=True).

Requires authentication as an organization account (account_type='organization').
Ordered by AURA score descending. Paginated.

schema.title: Response List Public Professionals Api Profiles Public Get

get.description: Get a public profile by username — no auth required.

post.description:
Record that an authenticated org viewed a professional's profile.

Sends an `org_view` notification to the professional — deduped: at most 1 notification
per (org, professional) pair per 24 hours. Silently returns 204 for non-org callers
(no error, no notification — safe to call from any authenticated user).

Security: org identity comes from JWT, never from request body.

get.description:
Count how many orgs viewed my profile (based on org_view notifications).

Returns: total views, views this week, list of org names who viewed (last 10).
Professional-only — orgs see their own dashboard stats elsewhere.

schema.title: Response Get My Profile Views Api Profiles Me Views Get

get.description:
Return coordinator ratings for this professional — these are expert verifications.

Data source: registrations where coordinator_rating is not null.
Joins with events (for event name) and profiles (for coordinator name/org).

schema.title: Response Get My Verifications Api Profiles Me Verifications Get

get.description: Get the current user's AURA score.

Get detailed explanation of AURA score — per-competency breakdown with evaluation logs.
Phase 2: Transparent Evaluation Logs — 'Show Your Work'.
DeCE detail (quote + confidence) included when available from LLM evaluations.

Route ordering: MUST be registered BEFORE /{volunteer_id} wildcard.
FastAPI matches routes in registration order — if the wildcard is first,
/me/explanation would match as volunteer_id="me" and return a 404.

Get any professional's AURA score (public profiles only, respects visibility).

Public endpoint — no auth required. Called by /u/[username] for anonymous visitors.
Rate limited to RATE_DISCOVERY (10/min) to prevent bulk enumeration scraping.

Uses service-role client (SupabaseAdmin) to check existence before enforcing
visibility — intentional for public discovery of non-hidden profiles.

Security (CRIT-04): Identical 404 response for hidden vs nonexistent profiles
to prevent profile existence enumeration attacks.

Profile view notifications are emitted via the dedicated endpoint:
POST /api/profiles/{username}/view — called explicitly by the frontend.

Route ordering: MUST come AFTER /me and /me/explanation — wildcard captures anything.

Get current AURA score visibility setting.

Settings page seeds the radio buttons from this endpoint — prevents
silently overriding the user's saved preference (Leyla simulation P0 fix).
Returns {"visibility": "public"|"badge_only"|"hidden"} or 404 if no score yet.

post.description: Grant or revoke sharing permission to an organization.

get.description:
Atlas personal reflection on user's AURA score.

Sprint Plan v2 Task 10 (E1). Returns 2-3 sentences of personalized
storytelling reflection. Cached per user per day (localStorage on frontend).
Uses Gemini Flash free tier (~$0.02/render).

schema.title: Response Get Atlas Reflection Api Aura Me Reflection Get

get.description: Return the caller's grievances ordered newest-first.

post.description:
File a formal grievance about an AURA score or assessment outcome.

The user owns the grievance. Admin review happens async; the user can poll
GET /api/aura/grievance to see status transitions (pending → reviewing →
resolved | rejected).

Audit note: this endpoint is the user-facing half of the ISO 10667-2 §7
obligation for automated employment decision systems. Every grievance is
persisted verbatim (no sanitization beyond length checks) so a regulator
can read the original complaint.

get.description:
Admin view: list grievances not yet resolved or rejected.

Sorted oldest-first so the queue is FIFO-ish (newest urgency first only
when SLA tiers differ, which we do not yet implement).

Admin view: list closed grievances (resolved + rejected).

Sorted newest-first so the most recent resolutions are at the top. Capped
at 200 rows per request. Used by the /admin/grievances history tab so
admins can review past decisions without scrolling.

patch.description:
Transition a grievance to reviewing / resolved / rejected.

Rules:
 • resolved + rejected are terminal — sets resolved_at timestamp
 • reviewing is transient — resolved_at stays NULL
 • resolution text is required when moving to resolved/rejected (otherwise
   the user gets a status change with no explanation — fails ISO 10667-2
   §7 transparency)

post.description: Start a new CAT session for a given competency.
Submit an answer to the current question and get the next item.
Finalise a session, run anti-gaming analysis, and upsert AURA score.

get.description: Retrieve results for a completed session.

post.description:
Generate personalised coaching tips for a completed assessment session.

Uses Gemini to produce 3 specific, actionable improvement tips based on
the volunteer's competency score. Result is cached in assessment_sessions.coaching_note
to avoid redundant LLM calls on repeat requests.

get.description:
Return metadata for a competency before the volunteer starts the assessment.

Includes time estimate, retake eligibility, and days until retake becomes available.
Used by the pre-assessment info page to show the volunteer what to expect.

Return per-question breakdown for a completed session.

Each question shows: text (EN/AZ), difficulty label, correctness, response time.
IRT parameters and raw scores are NEVER exposed (security audit CRIT-03).

Public verification endpoint — no auth required.

When someone clicks a shared badge link on LinkedIn, they land on a page
that calls this endpoint to prove the assessment is real.

Returns: competency, score, badge tier, questions answered, completion date.
Does NOT return: questions, answers, IRT parameters, theta.
List public open events — no auth required.

schema.title: Response List Events Api Events Get

post.description: Create an event — caller must own an organization.

get.description: Get a single event by ID.

put.description: Update an event — org owner only.

delete.description: Delete (cancel) an event — org owner only (RLS enforced).

post.description: Register the current user for an event.

Check in a participant via their QR code value.

SECURITY: Verifies caller is the org owner (event coordinator) before
allowing check-in. Without this, any authenticated user with a leaked
check-in code could check in participants.
Coordinator rates a participant after the event.
Participant rates an event after attending.

get.description: List registrations for an event — org owner only.

schema.title: Response List Registrations Api Events  Event Id  Registrations Get

get.description: Enriched attendee list — org owner only. Joins profiles + AURA scores.

schema.title: Response List Attendees Api Events  Event Id  Attendees Get

get.description: List the current user's event registrations.

schema.title: Response My Registrations Api Events My Registrations Get

get.description: List events owned by the caller's organization.

schema.title: Response List Events Api Eventshift Events Get

post.description: Create an event (org owner only).

schema.title: Response List Departments Api Eventshift Events  Event Id  Departments Get

get.description:
Return the operational blueprint (metadata) for a department.

The WUF13 GSE seed stores roles, SOPs, policies, FAQ, metrics, training,
and competencies inside department.metadata JSONB. This endpoint surfaces
that structure to clients, optionally filtered by section.

parameters.name: section

schema.description: Filter: roles, sops, policies, faq, metrics, training, competencies
schema.title: Section

parameters.description: Filter: roles, sops, policies, faq, metrics, training, competencies

schema.title: Response Get Department Blueprint Api Eventshift Departments  Department Id  Blueprint Get
Response List Areas Api Eventshift Departments  Department Id  Areas Get
Response List Units Api Eventshift Areas  Area Id  Units Get
Response List Assignments Api Eventshift Units  Unit Id  Assignments Get
Response List Metrics Api Eventshift Units  Unit Id  Metrics Get

get.description: Diagnostic: returns org_id + activation status for the caller.

schema.title: Response Debug Activation State Api Eventshift Debug Activation State Get

get.description: List all public organizations. Requires authentication to prevent unauthenticated enumeration.

schema.title: Response List Organizations Api Organizations Get

post.description: Create an organization for the current user.

get.description: Get the organization owned by the current user.

List all saved searches for the current user's organization.

Defined before /{org_id} to prevent route shadowing.

schema.title: Response List Saved Searches Early Api Organizations Saved Searches Get

post.description:
Save the current search filters under a name for later recall and match notifications.

Security:
- User must own an organization (org_id derived from JWT, never from body)
- Cap: max 20 saved searches per org
- Duplicate names rejected with 409

get.description: Get a public organization by ID. Requires authentication to prevent unauthenticated UUID enumeration.

Aggregated AURA talent pool metrics for an org's verified professionals.

Ownership-gated (403 for non-owners). Supports Collective AURA Ladders feature.
AURA is a global credential — a professional's score appears in all org pools they
engaged with (this is intentional, matching the platform's open credential model).

Aggregate stats for the B2B org management dashboard.

Returns assignment completion rates, avg AURA, badge distribution,
and top 5 professionals for the org owner's dashboard.

List all professionals assigned assessments by this org.

Returns profile + AURA data for each professional.
Supports filtering by session status (assigned/completed/in_progress).

schema.title: Response List Org Talent Api Organizations Me Professionals Get
Response Search Talent Api Organizations Search Professionals Post

post.description:
Assign competency assessments to specific professionals.

Security checks:
1. Caller must own an organization
2. Max 100 professionals per request (rate limit on endpoint too)
3. Competency slugs validated against DB
4. Duplicate assignments skipped (not error)

Send an introduction request from an org to a professional.

Requirements:
- Caller must be account_type='organization' (dual-checked: this endpoint + DB)
- Professional must have visible_to_orgs=True
- Only one pending request per org-professional pair (DB unique constraint)
Creates a notification for the professional.

patch.description:
Update the name or notify_on_match setting for a saved search.

Security: verifies the search belongs to the caller's org before updating.

delete.description:
Delete a saved search. Ownership verified before delete.

Security: double-check org ownership (search_id alone is not enough).
Returns 204 No Content on success.

post.description:
Upload a CSV of professionals to invite to the platform.

CSV format: email (required), display_name, phone, skills (pipe-delimited)
Max 500 rows. Duplicates within file and against existing invites are skipped.
Returns HTTP 207 with per-row audit log.

get.description: List invites for an organization, optionally filtered by status or batch.

schema.title: Response List Invites Api Organizations  Org Id  Invites Get

get.description: Return the expected CSV template for bulk invite.

Return an Open Badges 3.0 Verifiable Credential for a professional's AURA badge.

The credential is publicly accessible — no auth required.
Rate limited (RATE_DISCOVERY) to prevent profile enumeration.

schema.title: Response Get Open Badge Credential Api Badges  Professional Id  Credential Get

get.description: Open Badges issuer profile.

schema.title: Response Get Issuer Profile Api Badges Issuer Get

get.description:
Validate token and return professional + competency info for the rating UI.

Public endpoint — no auth header required.
Attacker note: only non-sensitive professional info is returned (display_name, username, avatar).
The token is single-use and time-limited — no brute-force risk beyond rate limiting.

post.description:
Submit a rating for a professional and mark the token as used.

This is atomic: we update token_used=TRUE in the same row as the rating.
If two concurrent requests hit this endpoint with the same token, the second
will get TOKEN_ALREADY_USED from _get_valid_token_row.
Note: There is a small TOCTOU window — mitigated by the UNIQUE constraint on
token + a DB-level check trigger if needed in production.

get.description:
Get activity feed for the current user.

Aggregates from assessment_sessions, badges, behavior_signals,
and event_registrations into a unified timeline.

schema.title: Response Get My Activity Api Activity Me Get

get.description:
Get dashboard stats for the current user.

Returns: events_attended, total_hours (estimated), verified_skills, streak_days.

schema.title: Response Get My Stats Api Activity Stats Me Get

post.description: Ingest a frontend analytics event. Fire-and-forget.

get.description:
Search public professional profiles by competency, score, role, and badge.

Returns cursor-paginated results. Use `next_after_score` + `next_after_id`
from meta to get the next page.

**Contact professionals:** Use the returned `professional_id` with
`POST /api/organizations/{org_id}/assign-assessments`.

Return three aggregate counts for landing / widget display.

Buckets count distinct professionals with at least one COMPLETED assessment
in the window. A single user who completed 3 competencies today still counts
as 1 in "today" (we use the user id as the aggregation key, not session count
— avoids making it look busier than reality).

Return recent lifesim events for the user — timeline rendering input.

Filters to lifesim_* event types (choice + crystal_spent) from
character_events so the client gets a clean Life Feed without all
other ecosystem noise.

Pick one eligible event from the pool for presentation.

Age + stats come from query params — frontend maintains session state
between chapters. Future iteration: aggregate from character_events
server-side to remove client-side truth dependence.

Returns None event if the pool yielded no eligible row; client should
show an empty-chapter state in that case.

parameters.name: money

schema.description: Unbounded — may be negative for debt
schema.title: Money

parameters.description: Unbounded — may be negative for debt

post.description:
Apply the chosen choice's consequences and log the event.

Validates event + choice exist in the pool, computes new stats via
pure-function helper, then fires the character_events emit (non-blocking).

Spend crystals on a Life Feed Crystal Shop item.

Validates item + balance, fires a crystal_spent event (for the shared
crystal ledger) AND a lifesim_crystal_spent event (for Life Feed surface
context). Client applies stat_boost optimistically; server is the truth.

get.description: Unread notification count — used for sidebar badge.
List user notifications, newest first.

patch.description: Mark all unread notifications as read.
Mark a single notification as read. Returns updated notification.

get.description:
Get platform-wide public statistics.

Returns professional count, completed assessments, active events, and average AURA score.
All counts use exact=True for accurate results. Designed to remain fast on free-tier.

Beta funnel health metrics — for the failure protocol.

SEC-Q3: Restricted to organization accounts only. Non-org users have no use for
this data, and exposing completion/abandonment rates to all users leaks operational
intelligence (platform health, abuse detection timing, user count signals).

Abandonment proxy: sessions with status='in_progress' AND updated_at < 24h ago.
This is not perfect (some users may still be in progress) but is a good signal.

post.description: Receive Telegram update via webhook. Uses Depends for admin client (OWASP HIGH-01 fix).

Register the webhook URL with Telegram API. Call once after deploy.

CRIT-03 FIX: Requires secret header to prevent unauthorized webhook re-registration.
Call with: curl -X POST .../api/telegram/setup-webhook -H "X-Admin-Secret: <TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET>"

Receive Sentry alert webhook, upsert fingerprint, escalate on recurrence.

Headers required:
    sentry-hook-signature  OR  x-sentry-signature  — HMAC-SHA256(body, secret)

Fail-closed when SENTRY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset (mirrors INC-008 pattern).

schema.title: Response Sentry Webhook Api Webhooks Sentry Post

post.description:
Store a character event from any product.

user_id is always sourced from the JWT — never from the request body.
Crystal events validate balance and idempotency BEFORE inserting.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's event history (paginated, newest first).

Params:
  limit  — hard cap 200 rows per request.
  offset — page offset (used with limit for history scroll).
  since  — ISO 8601 timestamp. If set, returns ONLY events created after
           this instant (newest first within the slice). Designed for
           incremental polling from another product (Life Sim, MindShift,
           BrandedBy) — they store the latest `created_at` they saw and
           pass it as `since=` on the next poll.

schema.title: Response List Character Events Api Character Events Get

get.description:
Compute and return current character state for the authenticated user.

Aggregates events + crystal ledger into a single snapshot via DB function.
Returns empty state for new users with no events.

Return crystal balance for the authenticated user.

Lightweight alternative to GET /state — used by MindShift to show the
crystal counter in the focus session overlay without the cost of the full
5-query get_character_state RPC.

Implementation: single SUM over game_crystal_ledger (indexed on user_id).
Get the current user's AI Twin (or null if none).

schema.title: Response Get My Twin Api Brandedby Twins Get

post.description: Create the user's AI Twin. One per user (MVP).

patch.description: Update the user's AI Twin. Only owner can update.

post.description:
Regenerate AI Twin personality from current character_state.

Reads verified skills, AURA scores, XP from character_events → generates
a personality prompt via Gemini → stores in ai_twins.personality_prompt.

This is BrandedBy's core moat: verified data → intelligent persona.

Activate the AI Twin (draft → active). Requires a photo and personality_prompt.

Once active, the twin can accept generation requests.

Request a new video/audio/chat generation.

If skip_queue=true, spends crystals via character_events ledger.

get.description: List the current user's generation jobs (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Generations Api Brandedby Generations Get

get.description: Get a single generation job by ID.
List all available product skills.

post.description:
Execute a product skill with user's context.

Loads the skill markdown, injects user data (profile, AURA, competencies),
calls LLM, returns structured output + Wave-3 audit fields.

get.description:
Return current subscription status for the authenticated user.

Auto-expires trials whose trial_ends_at has passed — writes the update
to the database so subsequent reads are consistent without a cron job.

post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout Session for the monthly subscription.

Flow:
1. Read profile (email + existing stripe_customer_id)
2. Create Stripe Customer if not already linked
3. Persist new stripe_customer_id immediately (before checkout — avoids orphans)
4. Create Checkout Session with customer pre-filled
5. Return {checkout_url}

Handle Stripe webhook events.

Security: validates Stripe-Signature header using stripe_webhook_secret.
Rejects any request that fails signature verification with 400.

Handled events:
- customer.subscription.created  → status = active
- customer.subscription.updated  → status = active | cancelled depending on Stripe status
- customer.subscription.deleted  → status = cancelled

get.description:
Return the user's current active tribe.

Returns null if user is not in an active tribe.
Members are returned with activity status only (no scores — anti-harassment).
Opted-out members are invisible.

schema.title: Response Get My Tribe Api Tribes Me Get

get.description:
Return the user's personal tribe streak.

Visible only to the user themselves (RLS: auth.uid() = user_id).
consecutive_misses_count is used by frontend for crystal fade animation.

schema.title: Response Get My Streak Api Tribes Me Streak Get

post.description:
Send anonymous kudos to your tribe.

Security: tribe_id comes from the user's own active membership (not user-supplied).
No sender_id stored. RLS INSERT policy verifies active membership.
Q1: count=0 → frontend shows "Be the first to send kudos" (handled in GET /me).

Leave the current tribe silently.

Q3: 2-person tribe continues until cycle end — remaining members are NOT notified.
Opted-out member's streak is NOT reset (streak is personal, tribe-independent).
No "X left the tribe" notification. Departed members are simply invisible in GET /me.

Request to renew the current tribe for another 4-week cycle.

If all active members request renewal → matching service will extend tribe on next run.

Opt into tribe matching (user signals they want to be matched).

Matching runs daily — user will be matched within 24 hours.
Eligibility: must have AURA score > 0 and visible_to_orgs = True.
If already in a tribe, returns current tribe info direction.

get.description:
Return whether the user is currently waiting in the matching pool.

Frontend uses this to show 'Finding your tribe...' across page refreshes
instead of re-showing the join CTA after the user already clicked it.

AdminGuard uses this to verify the caller is a platform admin.

Returns 200 for admins, 403 (from PlatformAdminId dep) for everyone else.
Intentionally minimal — just a gate, no data.

schema.title: Response Admin Ping Api Admin Ping Get

get.description: Platform health stats for the admin dashboard home page.

Tier-1 activation-first scorecard + Tier-2 cross-product presence matrix.

Tier 1 (5 cards): activation_rate_24h, w4_retention, dau_wau_ratio,
errors_24h, runway_months.
Tier 2: cross-product presence (VOLAURA only / MindShift only / both)
+ per-product activation funnels.

Live tail of character_events across the ecosystem — replaces Sankey viz.

CEO framing: "я хочу визуально видеть связи" → character_events IS the
cross-product connection log. Showing it as a stream is simpler and more
honest than rendering a Sankey at <100 users.

schema.title: Response Get Admin Live Events Api Admin Events Live Get

get.description: Paginated list of all platform users for the admin users page.

schema.title: Response List Admin Users Api Admin Users Get

get.description:
Organizations that have registered but not yet been verified.

Pending = is_active=True AND verified_at IS NULL.

schema.title: Response List Pending Organizations Api Admin Organizations Pending Get

post.description: Set verified_at = NOW() on an organization, granting it verified status.
Deactivate an organization (is_active=False). Reversible by re-approving.

get.description: Return all tracked agents from agent-state.json for the AI Office dashboard.

schema.title: Response Get Swarm Agents Api Admin Swarm Agents Get

get.description: Return swarm proposals from proposals.json for CEO review.

schema.title: Response Get Swarm Proposals Api Admin Swarm Proposals Get

post.description: CEO approves/dismisses/defers a swarm proposal.

schema.title: Response Decide Proposal Api Admin Swarm Proposals  Proposal Id  Decide Post

get.description:
Return typed FindingContract results from shared memory (SQLite blackboard).

These are findings from coordinator runs, simulate_users friction points,
and any agent that posted a result via post_result().

schema.title: Response Get Swarm Findings Api Admin Swarm Findings Get

post.description:
Manually trigger one batch of the 48h warm re-entry worker.

Returns a summary of candidates / sent / marked / errors so admin can see
immediately whether the kill switch is on (skipped_kill_switch > 0) or
actual sends happened.

Production cron should call this same path on a schedule (suggest hourly).

schema.title: Response Run Ghosting Grace Api Admin Ghosting Grace Run Post
Response Gateway Health Api Atlas Health Get
Response Receive Proposal Api Atlas Proposal Post

AITwinOut.title: AITwinOut
AITwinOut.description: Response model for an AI Twin.

AdminActivationFunnel.title: AdminActivationFunnel
AdminActivationFunnel.description: One product's activation funnel (signup → key action).

AdminActivityEvent.title: AdminActivityEvent
AdminActivityEvent.description: Single row in the live activity feed (character_events tail).

AdminOrgRow.title: AdminOrgRow
AdminOrgRow.description: Organization row for the admin approval queue.

AdminOverviewResponse.title: AdminOverviewResponse
AdminOverviewResponse.description:
Tier-1 exec scorecard + Tier-2 cross-product presence.

Activation-first founder dashboard: pre-PMF stage prioritises activation
and retention over MRR/NRR/CAC. See ADMIN-DASHBOARD-SPEC.md §7.

AdminPresenceMatrix.title: AdminPresenceMatrix
AdminPresenceMatrix.description: Cross-product presence counts. Replaces Sankey (premature at <100 users).

AdminStatsResponse.title: AdminStatsResponse
AdminStatsResponse.description: Platform health stats for the admin dashboard.

AdminUserRow.title: AdminUserRow
AdminUserRow.description: Minimal user row for the admin users table.

AnswerFeedback.title: AnswerFeedback
AnswerFeedback.description:
Immediate feedback after submitting an answer.

NOTE: raw_score intentionally NOT exposed to client (security audit CRIT-03).
Exposing BARS scores enables calibration attacks — users can reverse-engineer
what answer patterns score highest by submitting variations.

AssessmentInfoOut.title: AssessmentInfoOut
AssessmentInfoOut.description: Pre-assessment info shown before starting a competency assessment.

AssessmentResultOut.title: AssessmentResultOut
AssessmentResultOut.description:
Full results after session completion.

NOTE: theta/theta_se intentionally NOT exposed to client (security audit P1).
Exposing IRT ability estimates enables reverse-engineering the CAT algorithm.

AssignAssessmentRequest.title: AssignAssessmentRequest
AssignAssessmentRequest.description: Assign one or more competency assessments to specific professionals.

AuraCompetencyExplanation.title: AuraCompetencyExplanation
AuraCompetencyExplanation.description: Aggregated evaluation data for one competency session.

AuraEvaluationItem.title: AuraEvaluationItem
AuraEvaluationItem.description: Per-question evaluation log entry from a completed assessment session.

AuraExplanationResponse.title: AuraExplanationResponse
AuraExplanationResponse.description: Response for GET /aura/me/explanation — transparent per-competency breakdown.

BulkInviteResponse.title: BulkInviteResponse
BulkInviteResponse.description: Aggregate response for the bulk invite endpoint.

CharacterEventOut.title: CharacterEventOut
CharacterEventOut.description: Response for a stored character event.

crystal_balance.title: Crystal Balance
crystal_balance.description: Current crystal balance (floored at 0 — prevented from going negative at write time)

xp_total.title: Xp Total
xp_total.description: Lifetime XP earned across all products

verified_skills.title: Verified Skills
verified_skills.description: Volaura competency slugs verified by assessment, with score and badge tier

character_stats.title: Character Stats
character_stats.description: Life Simulator character stats — latest value per stat key (e.g. strength, intelligence)

CharacterStateOut.title: CharacterStateOut
CharacterStateOut.description:
Computed character state — derived from all events + crystal ledger.

This is the 'thalamus' view: one read shows the full cross-product state.
All four products read from this — Volaura, MindShift, Life Simulator, BrandedBy.

CollectiveAuraResponse.title: CollectiveAuraResponse
CollectiveAuraResponse.description: Aggregated AURA talent pool metrics for an org. Used by Collective AURA Ladders.

CreateVerificationLinkResponse.title: CreateVerificationLinkResponse
CreateVerificationLinkResponse.description: Returned when a verification link is created.

last_earned_at.title: Last Earned At
last_earned_at.description: When the last crystal was earned

lifetime_earned.title: Lifetime Earned
lifetime_earned.description: Lifetime crystals earned (positive ledger entries only)

CrystalBalanceOut.title: CrystalBalanceOut
CrystalBalanceOut.description:
Lightweight crystal balance — for MindShift quick-check without full state RPC.

Cheaper than GET /character/state: 1 SQL aggregate vs 5 serial queries.
MindShift reads this to show the crystal counter in the focus session overlay.

DiscoverableProfessional.title: DiscoverableProfessional
DiscoverableProfessional.description:
Professional profile visible to org users on the discovery page.

Only includes professionals who have opted in (visible_to_orgs=True).
AURA score joined from aura_scores table.

DiscoveryProfessional.title: DiscoveryProfessional
DiscoveryProfessional.description:
Single professional in discovery results.

Security (agent review 2026-03-25):
- display_name: server-side anonymized to "First L." — never trust user-controlled field
- competency_score: only the REQUESTED competency — not full competency_scores JSONB
- professional_id: exposed intentionally — used by POST /organizations/{id}/assign-assessments

DiscoveryResponse.title: DiscoveryResponse
DiscoveryResponse.description: Wrapped discovery response following {data, meta} envelope.

EventAttendeeRow.title: EventAttendeeRow
EventAttendeeRow.description: Enriched attendee for org dashboard — joins profile + AURA.

skip_queue.title: Skip Queue
skip_queue.description: If true, spend crystals to skip the queue

GenerationOut.title: GenerationOut
GenerationOut.description: Response model for a generation job.

GrievanceAdminOut.title: GrievanceAdminOut
GrievanceAdminOut.description:
Admin view includes user id + admin_notes + updated_at — fields intake
response does not expose to the owner.

InviteRowResult.title: InviteRowResult
InviteRowResult.description: Result for a single row in the bulk invite response.

message.title: Message
message.default: You have left the tribe. Find a new tribe to continue your streak.

OrgApproveResponse.title: OrgApproveResponse
OrgApproveResponse.description: Result of approving or rejecting an organization.

OrgDashboardStats.title: OrgDashboardStats
OrgDashboardStats.description: Aggregate stats for the org management dashboard.

OrgProfessionalRow.title: OrgProfessionalRow
OrgProfessionalRow.description: One professional row in the org dashboard table.

OrganizationResponse.title: OrganizationResponse
OrganizationResponse.description: Public organization response — owner_id intentionally excluded to prevent UUID enumeration.

PoolStatusOut.title: PoolStatusOut
PoolStatusOut.description:
Whether the user is currently waiting in the matching pool.

Used by GET /api/tribes/me/pool-status so the frontend can show
'Finding your tribe...' across page refreshes instead of the join CTA.

PublicProfileResponse.title: PublicProfileResponse
PublicProfileResponse.description: Minimal public profile for sharing — no sensitive fields.

PublicVerificationOut.title: PublicVerificationOut
PublicVerificationOut.description:
Public-facing assessment verification — shown when external viewer clicks shared badge link.

Intentionally minimal: proves the assessment happened, shows score + competency,
does NOT expose questions, answers, or IRT parameters.

QuestionBreakdownOut.title: QuestionBreakdownOut
QuestionBreakdownOut.description: Full per-question breakdown for a completed session.

QuestionOut.title: QuestionOut
QuestionOut.description: A single question as served to the frontend.

QuestionResultOut.title: QuestionResultOut
QuestionResultOut.description:
Per-question result shown after session completion.

NOTE: IRT parameters (irt_a/b/c) and raw_score intentionally NOT exposed.
difficulty_label is a mapped human-readable label from irt_b thresholds.
is_correct is derived from raw_score > 0 (binary, no numeric leak).

SavedSearchFilters.title: SavedSearchFilters
SavedSearchFilters.description:
Mirrors ProfessionalSearchRequest — the JSONB payload stored in org_saved_searches.filters.

Keeping this as a dedicated model (not reusing VolunteerSearchRequest) prevents drift:
saved filters never include pagination (limit/offset) and must be stable across versions.

SavedSearchOut.title: SavedSearchOut
SavedSearchOut.description: Response for a saved search row.

SessionOut.title: SessionOut
SessionOut.description:
Lightweight session state returned after start/answer.

NOTE: theta and theta_se are intentionally NOT exposed to the client.
Exposing IRT ability estimates enables reverse-engineering the CAT algorithm
and predicting upcoming questions. (Security audit P1, 2026-03-24)

SharingPermissionRequest.title: SharingPermissionRequest
SharingPermissionRequest.description: Grant/revoke sharing permission to an organization.

estimated_wait.title: Estimated Wait
estimated_wait.default: Your tribe will be matched within 24 hours.

TribeMatchPreview.title: TribeMatchPreview
TribeMatchPreview.description: Returned to user when they join the matching pool.

TribeMemberStatus.title: TribeMemberStatus
TribeMemberStatus.description: Public view of a tribe member — activity only, no scores.

TribeStreakOut.title: TribeStreakOut
TribeStreakOut.description: A user's personal tribe streak — visible only to themselves.

UpdateVisibilityRequest.title: UpdateVisibilityRequest
UpdateVisibilityRequest.description: Allow user to change their score visibility.

VerificationTokenInfo.title: VerificationTokenInfo
VerificationTokenInfo.description: Returned by GET /api/verify/{token} — public, no auth required.

VerifiedSkillOut.title: VerifiedSkillOut
VerifiedSkillOut.description: A single verified skill entry in the character state.

WebhookAck.title: WebhookAck
WebhookAck.description: Stripe webhook handler always returns this on success.
